images.caption: Figure 1:Length-normalized large-model probability vs. number of tokens in the first two reasoning steps for Qwen2.5-1.5B→\toQwen2.5-32B, averaged over all examples in each dataset. Longer reasoning steps are assigned systematically higher probabilities by the large model even after length normalization, making variable-length step scoring unreliable for likelihood-based guidance.

Figure 2:CGS accuracy vs. chunk lengthLLon GSM8K, MATH, and Minerva Math for both model pairs. The rightmost point (L=2048L{=}2048) corresponds to Best-of-NN. Performance is stable forL∈{10,20}L\in\{10,20\}across all datasets; MATH remains stable throughL=50L{=}50. All datasets degrade sharply atL=2048L{=}2048.
